quick_facts.label: Role scope
quick_facts.value: At the central-government level, the SSC Stenographer Grade ‘C’ and ‘D’ examination recruits stenographers for government ministries and departments.

quick_facts.label: Selection administration in DRDO
quick_facts.value: CEPTAM handles recruitment activities including vacancy advertising, application scrutiny, computer-based tests, trade tests and selection committees, then recommends final select lists to appointing authorities.

quick_facts.label: Promotion context
quick_facts.value: A 2025 SSC limited departmental examination notice provided routes from Stenographer Grade ‘D’ to Grade ‘C’/Personal Assistant-type roles in specified central secretariat and allied stenographer services, subject to cadre-specific service eligibility.

sections.heading: What a government stenographer does
paragraphs: The core work is to capture spoken dictation in shorthand and produce an accurate computer transcription under time limits. DRDO’s published skill-test instructions require candidates to take dictated material, review shorthand notes, transcribe on an allotted computer, and complete comparison and correction within the prescribed period—activities that closely reflect the accuracy- and deadline-sensitive work of the role.

In central-secretariat settings, stenographer services are intended to assist senior and top-level officers. In practice, this places stenographic work alongside confidential secretarial support, preparation of typed material and office coordination; the exact distribution of duties depends on the employing ministry, department, laboratory or cadre rules.
bullets: Take shorthand dictation and transcribe it accurately on a computer.
Review, compare and correct transcribed material within prescribed time limits.
Provide secretarial support to officers; assignment mix varies by organisation and posting.

sections.heading: Entry routes and eligibility
paragraphs: A broad central-government route is the SSC Stenographer Grade ‘C’ and ‘D’ examination, which the Staff Selection Commission describes as recruitment for stenographers in government ministries and departments. Candidates should use the live notice for the relevant examination cycle because eligibility, vacancies, age limits, language options, testing arrangements and department allocations can change.

For the DRDO-linked route represented by this node, CEPTAM has historically recruited Stenographer Grade-II in its Admin & Allied cadre. The CEPTAM-09 notice required a recognised 12th-class qualification, placed the post at an 18–27 age band subject to applicable relaxations, and used a computer-based Tier I followed by a mandatory qualifying dictation-and-transcription test. Treat these as an example from that advertisement, not as standing eligibility for a future DRDO vacancy.

CEPTAM is DRDO’s recruitment body, but a CEPTAM notification must actually include a stenographer post before it can be used as an entry route. For example, the currently listed CEPTAM-11 advertisement concerns STA-B and Technician-A positions, so applicants should not assume that every CEPTAM cycle recruits stenographers.
bullets: Monitor SSC notices for Grade ‘C’ and Grade ‘D’ direct-recruitment cycles.
Monitor DRDO/CEPTAM vacancy notices specifically for Admin & Allied stenographer openings.
Confirm the exact notification’s educational qualification, age cut-off, language, test format, vacancy count and posting conditions before applying.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The defining technical skill is rapid, accurate shorthand-to-computer transcription. DRDO’s published Stenographer Grade-II norm—80 words per minute dictation for 10 minutes followed by 50 minutes of English transcription—makes clear that speed alone is insufficient: candidates must also read shorthand notes reliably, type efficiently, and correct errors under pressure.

Computer literacy is also material to progression. The SSC’s 2025 limited departmental examination notice states that Stenographer Grade ‘D’ officials seeking promotion to Personal Assistant must transcribe shorthand notes on computers. Strong English usage, careful listening, proofreading, document formatting and discretion are therefore practical complements to shorthand and typing ability.
bullets: English shorthand dictation and transcription practice.
Fast, accurate computer typing and proofreading.
Listening concentration, grammar and punctuation control.
Computer-based document production and confidential office handling.

sections.heading: Workplace and posting conditions
paragraphs: Central-government stenographers can be allocated to different ministries, departments and offices through SSC selection, with final placement linked to the applicable recruitment process and candidate preferences where the notice provides for them.

For DRDO’s Admin & Allied recruitment, the CEPTAM-09 notice stated that selected candidates could be appointed anywhere within the Union of India, including field or remote locations, according to organisational requirements. The same notice listed multiple possible stations and stated a minimum five-year period at the first posting, while allowing public-interest transfers. These are dated DRDO-notice conditions and may differ in a later recruitment cycle.
bullets: Office-based work is typical, supporting officers and organisational units.
Central-government postings may vary by allocated department or office.
DRDO postings can be geographically mobile and organisation-driven.

sections.heading: Progression pathways
paragraphs: Progression is cadre-specific rather than automatic. The SSC’s 2025 Grade ‘C’ Stenographers Limited Departmental Competitive Examination covered several services, including the Central Secretariat Stenographers Service, Indian Foreign Service Branch (B), Armed Forces Headquarters, Railway Board Secretariat, Election Commission and Central Vigilance Commission stenographer services.

In the Central Secretariat Stenographers Service example, regularly appointed Grade ‘D’ stenographers could seek promotion through the limited departmental examination after meeting the notice’s approved-service conditions; the 2025 notice generally specified six years, with a stated proviso for certain competitively appointed officials. Other named cadres applied different qualifying-service requirements. A move toward Personal Assistant is therefore possible, but depends on the employee’s service/cadre rules, length of approved service, examination and vacancies.
bullets: Build a record of accurate transcription, computer proficiency and reliable secretarial work.
Track the departmental/limited competitive examinations applicable to the specific cadre.
Check cadre-specific service-length rules and training requirements rather than relying on a single promotion timeline.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Compensation differs by employer, grade, city and the applicable government orders. As a concrete but dated DRDO example, the CEPTAM-09 Admin & Allied advertisement listed Stenographer Grade-II at 7th CPC Pay Level 4 with a basic-pay range of ₹25,500–81,100. It also described eligibility for allowances and facilities under Government of India rules, including dearness allowance, house-rent allowance, transport allowance and medical facilities, and stated National Pension System coverage unless otherwise provided by rules.

This should not be read as a current take-home-pay estimate or as a pay guarantee for all government stenographer posts. Candidates should use the pay level, allowances, pension terms, city classification and conditions stated in the particular appointment advertisement and current Department of Expenditure orders.
bullets: Use the vacancy notice’s pay level and pay matrix as the starting point.
Treat basic pay, allowances and take-home pay as distinct figures.
Verify current allowance and pension rules at the time of application or appointment.
